titleStmt.title: SII 18.334: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 334. (B.K. No. 12 of 1932-33.) DĒVAGĒRI, HAVERI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Two pillars set up in front of the temple of Yellamma.

summary.p: This inscription is dated in Śaka 987, Viśvāvasu, Bhādrapada śu. Shashṭi, Monday. The tithi corresponds to A.D. 1065, August 10, Wednesday (not Monday).

It registers a grant of land made by Mahāsāmanta Rājādityarasa of the Jīmūtavāhaṇa lineage and Khachara family to the god Gōyindēśvaradēva. The gift was entrusted to Gaṅgarāśi-Munipa. Rājāditya is stated to be holding the manneya of Bāsavūra One-hundred-and-forty.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti samastavasumatītaḷakhyāta
jīmotavāhanānvayaprasūtaṁ bandhu
janakamaḻinīrājahaṁsaṁ khacaravaṁsōttaṁsaṁ
padmāvatīlabdhavaraprasāda nāmādisamastapra
sastisahitaṁ śrīmanmahāsāmaṁntarājā
dityarasaṁ bāsavura(ṁ)nūṟanālvattakkaṁ manne
yadarasugeyyuttamire . v® . Idirāṁtaṁgeredaṁge
bandu saraṇe(ṁ)ndaṁgājiyoḷu rāmanōvade pūṇdīve
ḍeyoḷu dinēśasutanudyadbhītaraṁ kāyalārppadaṭiṁ
khēcararājanendu dharaṇīcakraṁ manōrāgadiṁ viditē
śāsaspadanaṁ vivēkanidhi rājādityanaṁ baṇniku . kaṁ . lalane
pugala(ṁ)ṇmareyde rājiya kī
rttilalane kaḍugāḷe naguvaḷu jalanidhiyaṁ dāṁṭi sakaḷadi
kpāḷakaroḷ .

sakavariṣa 987 neya(vi)visvāvasusaṁvatsarada bhādrapada
suddha cchaṭṭi sōmavāradaṁdu mahāsāvantaṁ rājādityara
saru svasti yamaniyamasvādhyāyadhyānadhāraṇamō
nānuṣṭhāṇajapasamādhiśīḷaguṇasaṁpannarenisida . kaṁ .
parahitacaritarigaghakuḷahararige madamānahariṣa
varjjitarige ṣaḻdarisanapariṇatarige gaṁgārāsimunipari
ge negardda rājādityaṁ (.) kālaṁ kaḻci dhārāpūrvvakaṁ māḍi gōyi
ndēśvaradēvargge tāṇilageṟeya baḍagaṇa kōḍiyiṁ baḍagalu
sarvvābhyantarasiddhiyiṁ namasyamāge biṭṭa Eremattaru paṁnne
raḍu caṭṭēśvaradēvargge biṭṭa Erematturāṟu mattaṁ gōyindēśvara
dēvargge konnigeṟeya paḍuvaṇa baccaliṁ baḍaga Eremattaroṁ
ndu ©
Digital edition of SII 18.334 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
